;<span id="Ordinal_number">ordinal number</span>
:number assigned to each [[Glossary:Element|element]] indicating the designed ordinal position of the element in the [[Glossary:Collection|collection]]. Ordinals are important when writing commercial collections. Instead of ordinals, users should use priorities in their own collections. Some collections are sorted by their '''ordinal numbers''' and this is the order in which they should usually be learned. '''Ordinal number''' comes with a [[Glossary:Collection|collection]], while the [[Glossary:Priority|priority]] is set for each [[Glossary:Element|element]] by the user using his or her own criteria. By default, '''ordinal number''' is set to the number of [[Glossary:Element|elements]] in the current [[Glossary:Collection|collection]] plus 10,000. This way, the first [[Glossary:Element|element]] you add will have the '''ordinal''' 10,002, second element will get 10,003, etc. '''Ordinal numbers''' are useful in sorting commercial [[Glossary:Collection|collections]] or their [[Glossary:Element_subset|subsets]]
